name: Vegetable Kabobs
recipeIngredient: 1 Small eggplant, about 3/4 lb., cut into 2-inch cubes
1 Large red or green pepper, seeded and cut into 1-inch squares
2 Medium onions, cut into wedges (separate wedges into 2-3 layers each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook eggplant in boiling water for 3 minutes, then drain.
2. Cook carrots in boiling water until just tender crisp, then drain.
3. Cook unpeeled potatoes in boiling water for approximately 15-20 minutes; drain and halve them.
4. Place eggplant, carrots, potatoes, zucchini, peppers, onions, and mushrooms into a large plastic bag. Add marinade, seal the bag well, and refrigerate for 2 hours or overnight.
5. Drain vegetables and reserve the marinade. Using 8 metal skewers, thread the vegetables, alternating varieties. Place on a lightly greased grill over low heat.
6. Cook, turning often and basting with the reserved marinade, for 10-15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
7. Sprinkle lightly with salt before serving. The remaining marinade can be used again and refrigerated for up to 2 weeks.
8. NOTE: If wooden skewers are used, soak them in water for 15-20 minutes before using to prevent burning.
